description: Running a destructive, irreversible migration step (drop/truncate/transform) with no tested backup or rollback path.
body: Failure mode: a migration drops a column, truncates a table, or transforms data in place with no backup and no down migration, so a bug or bad assumption causes permanent data loss. Detection signals: the migration has no reverse step; no fresh backup precedes it; the change is applied directly to production without a rehearsal. Do not run an irreversible destructive step unguarded. Safer fallback: take and verify a backup, prefer expand-contract so the old data survives until proven unneeded, rehearse on a production-like copy, and gate the destructive step behind explicit human approval and a confirmed point-of-no-return.
